name: @abtree/docs-maintainer

steps.instruct: Read the CLI source rooted at $GLOBAL.cli_root and enumerate the full public surface: every command, subcommand, positional, flag, option, environment variable, and exit code a user is expected to see. Be exhaustive — this list is the source of truth for documentation completeness. Save the enumeration as a structured object at $LOCAL.cli_surface keyed by command path, each entry containing: - command (full invocation path) - summary (one line) - args (positionals) - flags (with type and default) - env (environment variables read) - exit_codes - source_path (where the surface is defined)

Walk $GLOBAL.docs_root. For every documentation page (markdown, MDX, or doc-config file) capture: - path - tier: "orientation" | "education" | "reference" (orientation = top-level pages such as home / motivation / getting-started; education = guides, tutorials, conceptual explainers; reference = lookup pages, schemas, CLI reference) - purpose: one sentence on what the page is for - audience: who reads it - clarity_score: 0..1 (does the prose read well?) - impact_score: 0..1 (how load-bearing is the page in the reader's journey?) - srp_score: 0..1 (does the page own exactly one concept, task, or surface?) - issues: list of concrete problems flagged on this page (style, structure, gaps, drift) Save the full inventory at $LOCAL.inventory.

Compose a markdown sitemap from $LOCAL.inventory. Structure: --- private: true generated_by: docs-maintainer generated_at: <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM> --- # Documentation sitemap ## Tier map Three sections — Orientation, Education, Reference — each listing the pages in that tier with a one-line summary. Within a tier, order pages by impact_score descending. ## Per-page detail For each page in $LOCAL.inventory: ### <path> - Tier: <tier> - Purpose: <purpose> - Audience: <audience> - Scores: clarity <n> | impact <n> | SRP <n> - Issues: bulleted list (or "none") - Status: "pending" | "amended" | "stable" ## Narrative arc One paragraph describing the intended reader journey from orientation → education → reference, naming the specific pages that anchor each stage. Write the markdown to ./documentation-sitemap.md (overwriting any prior copy). Save the path to $LOCAL.sitemap. This file is private — it is the maintainer's working document, not a published page, and the frontmatter must declare `private: true`.

If $LOCAL.initial_sitemap is already set, this is not the first cycle — submit success without doing anything. Otherwise copy the file at $LOCAL.sitemap to ./documentation-sitemap.initial.md (overwriting any prior copy) and save the new path to $LOCAL.initial_sitemap. This is the only record of the initial state, since Build_Sitemap overwrites the live sitemap on every cycle.

Read fragments/style-principles.md verbatim and apply it to the sitemap at $LOCAL.sitemap and the underlying docs at $GLOBAL.docs_root. Act as a senior technical writer assessing the site against: - MDN three-tier structure (orientation / education / reference) - Microsoft Manual of Style (voice, tense, sentence case, parallel construction, action-oriented headings, scannable structure, defined jargon) - Narrative arc — does the reader progress orientation → education → reference cleanly? - Single responsibility — does each page own exactly one concept? - Coverage — is the educational content sufficient to onboard a new reader end-to-end? Emit an ordered catalog of suggestions stored at $LOCAL.suggestions, highest-impact first. Each suggestion: { id, target_path, type, severity, description, why } where `type` is one of: "split", "merge", "rewrite", "rename", "move", "add", "delete", "restructure". An empty list is a valid result and signals the cycle is converging. Set $LOCAL.suggestions_count to the length of $LOCAL.suggestions.

If $LOCAL.suggestions is empty, set $LOCAL.current_suggestion to null and submit success — there is nothing to apply this pass. Otherwise pop the head of $LOCAL.suggestions into $LOCAL.current_suggestion and remove it from the queue.

If $LOCAL.current_suggestion is null, submit success — the queue is empty and there is nothing to apply. Otherwise apply $LOCAL.current_suggestion. Edit the target file(s) at $LOCAL.current_suggestion.target_path. Consult fragments/style-principles.md as you write so the new text matches the encoded MDN + Microsoft Manual of Style principles. Then amend $LOCAL.sitemap inline: locate the per-page entry for $LOCAL.current_suggestion.target_path and update its Status to "amended", refresh its scores, and add a one-line note under Issues describing what landed. If the suggestion created or deleted pages, add or remove the corresponding entries in the sitemap and update the Tier map. Append a summary of the applied suggestion to $LOCAL.applied_log: { id, target_path, type, summary }.

Cross-check $LOCAL.cli_surface against the docs under $GLOBAL.docs_root. For every command, flag, environment variable, and exit code in the CLI surface, locate where it is documented (path + line). Compose a coverage report at $LOCAL.coverage_report: { documented: [...], missing: [...], stale: [...] } where `missing` is anything in $LOCAL.cli_surface not found in the docs, and `stale` is anything documented that no longer exists in $LOCAL.cli_surface. Set $LOCAL.coverage_complete to true iff `missing` and `stale` are both empty.

Run the docs-review procedure documented at fragments/docs-review.md. Read that file and follow its numbered steps verbatim, scoping the review to the changes captured in $LOCAL.applied_log during this cycle. The procedure orchestrates haiku/sonnet/opus subagents in parallel and ends with a filtered, high-signal issue list. Capture that final list (issue description + reason + cited_lines + page_path) as $LOCAL.review_report. An empty list is a valid result — it is the signal that this cycle's changes hold up.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.suggestions_count is 0 and $LOCAL.coverage_complete is true and $LOCAL.review_report is empty

steps.instruct: Cycle converged: no outstanding critique, full CLI coverage, and no review findings. The documentation set is now clear, consistent, and progressively structured.

steps.evaluate: $LOCAL.initial_sitemap is set and $LOCAL.sitemap is set and $LOCAL.applied_log is set

steps.instruct: Compose a markdown change report. Required sections: # Documentation change report — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM> ## Headline - Pages reviewed: <n> - Suggestions applied: <count of $LOCAL.applied_log> - CLI coverage: complete | <n missing> | <n stale> - Final review findings: <count of $LOCAL.review_report> ## What changed Bulleted list rendered from $LOCAL.applied_log: target → type → summary. ## Coverage $LOCAL.coverage_report (verbatim). ## Final review $LOCAL.review_report (verbatim). ## Sitemaps on disk - Initial: $LOCAL.initial_sitemap - Final: $LOCAL.sitemap Write the markdown to ./DOCUMENTATION_CHANGE_REPORT.md (overwriting any prior copy). Save the path to $LOCAL.change_report.

description: Sweep a docs site, classify every page into the MDN orientation / education / reference tiers, score each on clarity, impact, and single responsibility, and gather everything into a private documentation-sitemap.md. A senior-writer critique drives a bounded refactor loop that applies suggestions and amends the sitemap inline. Each cycle ends with a CLI completeness gate and a multi-agent review; cycles repeat until the site is clear, consistent, fully covers the CLI, and the review is clean.

global.docs_root: the docs directory of the project being maintained (e.g. ./docs)
global.cli_root: the source directory of the CLI being documented (e.g. ./packages/cli)
